metadata.source: College websites and historical admissions data
metadata.description: Estimated admissions decision notification dates for the 2025-2026 cycle
metadata.note: Dates are approximate based on historical patterns. Actual dates may vary by a few days each year. All dates reference when online portals update, not mailed letters.
metadata._verificationNotes: Application deadlines (Nov 1, Jan 1, etc.) are well-established and accurate. Notification dates are approximate (e.g., 'Mid-December', 'Late March') based on historical patterns and may shift by a few days each year. Commitment deadline of May 1 is standard for most schools. Users should check individual school portals for exact dates.

schools.notes: Harvard uses Restrictive Early Action (REA), not ED. REA notifications typically mid-December. RD decisions historically around late March/early April. Class of 2030 RD expected ~March 28.
Yale uses Single-Choice Early Action (SCEA). EA notifications mid-December. RD typically late March/early April. Admitted students reply by May 1.
ED I decisions mid-December. ED II decisions mid-February. RD decisions typically late March/early April.
Princeton uses Single-Choice Early Action (SCEA). Notifications mid-December. RD decisions late March. Princeton has no binding early option.
ED I decisions mid-December. ED II for some colleges. RD decisions typically late March/early April. Decisions may vary by college within Cornell.
ED decisions mid-December. Penn does not offer ED II or EA. RD decisions typically late March/early April.
ED decisions mid-December. Brown does not offer ED II or EA. RD decisions typically late March/early April.
ED decisions mid-December. Dartmouth does not offer ED II or EA. RD decisions typically late March/early April.
Stanford uses Restrictive Early Action (REA). REA notifications mid-December. RD decisions typically late March/early April (~April 1).
MIT uses Early Action (non-restrictive). EA decisions mid-December (~Dec 14). RD decisions mid-March (~March 14, Pi Day tradition).
UChicago offers ED I (Dec), ED II (Feb), and EA (mid-Jan). RD decisions late March. One of few elite schools with both EA and ED.
Notre Dame uses Restrictive Early Action (REA). REA decisions mid-December. RD decisions typically late March.
ED decisions mid-December. Northwestern does not offer EA or ED II. RD decisions typically late March (~March 25-28).
ED decisions mid-December. Duke does not offer ED II or EA. RD decisions typically late March/early April.
ED I decisions mid-December. ED II decisions mid-February. RD decisions typically late March.
ED decisions mid-December. Rice does not offer ED II or EA. RD decisions typically late March/early April.
ED I decisions mid-December. ED II decisions mid-February. RD decisions typically late March.
ED I decisions mid-December. ED II decisions mid-February. RD decisions typically late March/early April.
Georgetown uses Early Action (non-restrictive). EA decisions typically mid-December. RD decisions typically late March/early April.
ED I decisions mid-December. ED II decisions mid-February. RD decisions typically late March.
ED I decisions mid-December (~Dec 15). ED II decisions mid-February. RD decisions typically late March/early April.
ED I decisions mid-December. EA decisions mid-January. ED II decisions mid-February. RD decisions late March. Known for generous merit aid.
USC does not offer EA or ED. All decisions are Regular Decision, typically released late March (~March 25). Scholarship notifications follow.
ED I decisions mid-December. ED II decisions mid-February. RD decisions typically late March/early April.
ED I decisions mid-December. ED II decisions mid-February. RD decisions typically late March.
ED decisions mid-December. EA decisions mid-February. RD decisions late March/early April.
ED I decisions mid-December. ED II decisions mid-February. RD decisions typically late March/early April.
ED I decisions mid-December. EA decisions mid-January. ED II decisions mid-February. RD decisions late March.
EA decisions mid-January. RD decisions typically late March/early April.
ED I decisions mid-December. ED II decisions mid-February. RD decisions typically late March.
EA decisions late December. RD decisions typically late March. Rolling thereafter.
ED I decisions mid-December. ED II decisions mid-February. RD decisions late March.
ED I decisions as early as late November. EA decisions late January. ED II decisions mid-February. RD decisions late March.
ED decisions mid-December. EA decisions mid-January. RD decisions late March.
ED decisions mid-December. EA decisions mid-January. RD decisions late March/early April.
ED I mid-December. EA mid-January. ED II mid-February. RD late March.
ED I decisions mid-December. ED II decisions mid-February. RD decisions late March.
EA decisions mid-January. RD decisions late March. Rolling admissions available.
ED I mid-December. ED II mid-February. RD late March.
ED I mid-December. EA late January. ED II mid-February. RD late March.
ED I mid-December. ED II mid-February. RD late March.
UC Berkeley releases decisions in late March (~March 25-28). UC system uses a single application with no EA/ED options.
UCLA releases decisions in late March (~March 20-25). UC system uses a single application. Historically one of the first UCs to release.
UC Davis releases decisions mid-March (~March 10-15). Often among the first UC campuses to release decisions.
UC Irvine releases decisions mid-to-late March. UC system application.
UCSD releases decisions mid-March (~March 12-18). UC system application.
UCSB releases decisions mid-March. UC system application.
UMN uses rolling admissions with priority deadline. Most decisions out by late January for fall applicants.
EA decisions typically late January/early February. RD decisions late March/early April. EA is highly competitive.
EA decisions late January/early February. RD decisions late March/early April.
EA decisions late January/early February. RD decisions late February/early March. Engineering and CS are particularly competitive.

schools.school: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
schools.notes: EA decisions late January. RD decisions late March. Strong in-state preference.
EA decisions mid-February. Priority deadline decisions by late February. Rolling after that. Auto-admit for top 6% of Texas high schools.
EA decisions as early as mid-November. RD decisions late February/early March. Georgia has one of the earliest EA notification dates.
UW Seattle releases decisions mid-March (~March 1-15). Single application deadline in November. No EA/ED options.
EA decisions late January. RD decisions on rolling basis through late March.
UF releases decisions in mid-February. Single deadline, no EA/ED. Known for being one of the earliest to release.
FSU releases decisions starting mid-February on a rolling basis.
ASU uses rolling admissions. Most students hear within 2-4 weeks of applying. Very accessible admissions process.
EA decisions mid-January. RD decisions late February/early March. Engineering is particularly competitive.
EA decisions late January. RD decisions late February. Rolling thereafter.
Penn State uses rolling admissions. Decisions typically within 4-6 weeks of completed application.
Rutgers releases decisions starting late January on a rolling basis through March.
Decisions released on rolling basis, most by late February/early March.
EA decisions late January. RD decisions late March/early April.
UConn releases decisions on a rolling basis, most by late February.
EA decisions mid-January. RD decisions late February. Rolling thereafter.
University of Iowa uses rolling admissions. Most decisions within 2-3 weeks.
EA decisions mid-December. RD decisions late February/early March.
IU Bloomington uses rolling admissions with priority consideration deadline. Decisions typically within 3-4 weeks.
EA decisions late January. RD decisions late March.
Most decisions released by late January for priority applicants. Rolling admissions available.
EA decisions mid-December. RD decisions late February/early March.
Pitt uses rolling admissions. Decisions typically within 3-4 weeks of completed application.
EA decisions mid-January (~Jan 14-18). RD decisions mid-March. Engineering-heavy admits may come in waves.
Most decisions by mid-February. Priority deadline in December.
ED decisions mid-December. RD decisions late March/early April.
